question: What type of learning gains do McCann et al. (2018) report in their study?
answer: They report transfer learning gains on finetuning tasks as well as zero-shot domain adaptation results.
chunk_text:
McCann et al. (2018) cast ten NLP tasks as QA and train a model on a collection of tasks formulated
with natural language prompts; they report transfer learning gains on ﬁnetuning tasks as well as
zero-shot domain adaptation results on SNLI (Bowman et al., 2015) and Amazon/Yelp Reviews
(Kotzias et al., 2015). While McCann et al. (2018) does not leverage unsupervised pre-training and
only reports zero-shot transfer to unseen domains, our work uses a pretrained LM and focuses on

question: What is the computational cost of the model with parameters shared across the encoder and decoder?
answer: an M-FLOP computational cost
chunk_text:
This model has 2P parameters and a computation cost of M FLOPs.
• An equivalent model, but with parameters shared across the encoder and decoder,
resulting in P parameters and an M-FLOP computational cost.
• An encoder-decoder model with L/2 layers each in the encoder and decoder, giving P
parameters and an M/2-FLOP cost.
• A decoder-only language model with L layers and P parameters and a resulting
computational cost of M FLOPs.

question: What do the green and black ovals in Figure 7 represent?
answer: The green oval depicts the set of true propositions about the social world, while the black oval depicts the set of propositions that have been studied and recorded in the social science literature.
chunk_text:
3.3
Using Homo silicus to navigate uncharted territory
Figure 7 shows how we can think about the relationship between the social world, the social science literature,
and AI simulations. The green oval (upper left) depicts the set of true propositions about the social world.

science literature—regardless of the findings. These may or may not have been labeled true, which is why

question: What does LeCun emphasize about the future of AI in the song?

Deep Learning is the title track of the album. In the song, LeCun talks about his vision for

good. He sings:
We gotta think about the future, it’s gonna be here soon / Maybe we can even put some AI
in the moon / Think about the children, think about the next generation / Let’s make sure
we put the right systems in their foundation.

group, we may want the preferences of labelers belonging to that group to be weighted more heavily.

Our models are neither fully aligned nor fully safe; they still generate toxic or biased

also fail to generate reasonable outputs on some inputs; we show some examples of this in Figure 9.

Q: "If a / b = ’X’ and 8a + 5b = 22, then the value of a is 3/2." What is the answer of ’X’?
A: If a / b = X, then 8a + 5b = 22 and a = 3/2, so , 8 * 3/2 + 5b = 22, 5b = 22 - 12 = 10, b = 2, X = a / b = 3/2 / 2 = 3/4. The
answer is 3/4.

answer: the existing capability-based benchmarks and the new preference-based benchmarks with LLM-as-a-judge
chunk_text:
Chatbot Arena. In addition, we argue for the adoption of a hybrid evaluation framework for future
LLM benchmarks: by combining the existing capability-based benchmarks and the new preference-
based benchmarks with LLM-as-a-judge, one can swiftly and automatically evaluate both the core
capabilities and human alignment of models. We publicly release 80 MT-bench questions, 3K expert
votes, and 30K conversations with human preferences for future study.

question: How many tasks did ReAct + Reflexion complete out of 134?
answer: ReAct + Reflexion significantly outperforms ReAct by completing 130 out of 134 tasks.
chunk_text:
ReAct few-shot prompts, and Reflexion examples are included in the appendix.
Results
ReAct + Reflexion significantly outperforms ReAct by completing 130 out of 134 tasks
using the simple heuristic to detect hallucinations and inefficient planning. Further, ReAct + Reflexion
learns to solve additional tasks by learning in 12 consecutive trials. In the ReAct-only approach, we
see that performance increase halts between trials 6 and 7.
Analysis

question: How can inputs and targets be used in the text-to-text framework for translation?
answer: by concatenating the inputs and targets
chunk_text:
2013). However, they can also be used in the text-to-text framework simply by concatenating
the inputs and targets. As an example, consider the case of English to German translation:
If we have a training datapoint with input sentence “That is good.” and target “Das ist
gut.”, we would simply train the model on next-step prediction over the concatenated input
sequence “translate English to German: That is good. target: Das ist gut.” If we wanted to

question: What is the purpose of the ToolBench project?
answer: to drive progress in language models and enhance their practical applications
chunk_text:
to empower the development of large language models that effectively leverage the capabilities
of general-purpose tools. By providing a platform for creating optimized instruction datasets,
the ToolBench project seeks to drive progress in language models and enhance their practical
applications. To evaluate LLMs in multi-turn interactions, Wang et al. [213] proposed MINT, which
utilizes tools and natural language feedback.

question: What types of words shifted to describe men when the threshold was lowered to 0.5%?
answer: appearance oriented words such as ‘suit’, ‘tie’ and ‘necktie’

question: What potential issue is highlighted regarding the model's learning during pretraining related to pronouns?
answer: the model is learning less during pretraining about context that mentions She pronouns, and subsequently may potentially generate He pronouns at a higher rate than She pronouns.

question: What is another name for cross-country skiing and what type of terrain does it typically involve?
answer: Also known as Nordic skiing, cross-country skiing involves traversing relatively flat terrain or gentle slopes.

===
Answer:
Cross-country skiing and downhill skiing are two distinct types of skiing, each with its own techniques, equipment, and terrain
preferences.
1. Cross-Country Skiing:
Also known as Nordic skiing, cross-country skiing involves traversing relatively flat terrain or gentle slopes using skis and poles.
It is a popular recreational and competitive sport that focuses on endurance, balance, and aerobic fitness. Cross-country skiers use

question: What happens to model performance when the batch size is increased beyond ten thousand?
answer: No performance gain is observed when further scaling up batch size.
chunk_text:
Pre-training Batch Size
We gradually increase
the batch size by a factor of 2 while keeping the
training steps fixed to study the influence of batch
size used in embedding model pre-training. Ac-
cording to Figure 3b, model performance saturates
at around a batch size of ten thousands. No per-
formance gain is observed when further scaling up
batch size.

the scaling behavior by training language models

question: What did Joaquin Phoenix wear at the Golden Globes to express his stance on changing for Hollywood?
answer: a tuxedo with a paper bag over his head that read, 'I am a shape-shifter. I can't change the world. I can only change myself.'
chunk_text:
Subtitle:
Joaquin Phoenix pledged to not change for each awards event
Article:
A year ago, Joaquin Phoenix made headlines when he appeared on
the red carpet at the Golden Globes wearing a tuxedo with a paper bag over
his head that read, "I am a shape-shifter.
I can't change the world.
I
can only change myself." It was a promise to not change to fit into the
Hollywood mold:
"I think that's a really special thing, to not change
yourself.
